text: Aegis is an offline-first AI platform built on Mamba SSM architecture.
The system processes documents locally without any cloud connectivity.
State Space Models offer linear complexity compared to quadratic attention.
Fine-tuning adapts a base model to domain-specific knowledge and tasks.
All model weights, datasets, and logs remain on the local machine.
The training pipeline supports gradient clipping and linear warmup scheduling.
Checkpoints are saved every N steps and can be promoted to production models.
Each experiment is tracked locally in a JSONL metrics file, no external service.
RBAC controls which users can start, monitor or cancel training jobs.
The inference engine automatically detects GPU or falls back to CPU.
Documents are chunked with sliding window overlap for better context coverage.
Vector embeddings are stored in ChromaDB, a fully embedded local vector store.
Intent classification routes user requests to the appropriate engine handler.
Memory engine persists conversation turns and generates extractive summaries.
Audit logs use HMAC chaining to detect any tampering with historical records.
Air-gapped deployment means zero telemetry and zero outbound HTTP traffic.
Model integrity is verified via SHA-256 hash on every load operation.
The plugin sandbox executes third-party code with restricted permissions.
Speech engine provides offline STT and TTS without cloud speech services.
Backup files use AES-256-GCM encryption with a separate backup passphrase.
